proposed_reference.white_box_l_bfgs_note: See adversarial-vit-cifar10_results.json for exact-secret optimization results; the proposed transform is not a lossless permutation and does not have a closed-form inverse.

scheme_results.label: PE-style pixel negative/channel shuffle
scheme_results.key_recovery_note: exact with known key; chosen plaintext can recover per-pixel signs/channel order

scheme_results.label: EtC-style block scrambling
scheme_results.key_recovery_note: exact with known key; chosen plaintext can recover block/pixel/channel operations

scheme_results.label: block-wise random orthogonal transform
scheme_results.key_recovery_note: exact with known key; chosen plaintext basis probes can recover the orthogonal matrix

scheme_results.label: unrestricted ViT block+pixel permutation
scheme_results.key_recovery_note: one unique calibration image recovers the global permutation when the same key is reused

scheme_results.label: patch-local pixel permutation only
scheme_results.key_recovery_note: one unique calibration image recovers the in-patch permutation when the same key is reused

scheme_results.label: Horio restricted setting Nbs=120,Nps=500
scheme_results.key_recovery_note: one unique calibration image recovers the restricted permutation when the same key is reused

scheme_results.label: restricted per-image/key permutation example Nbs=147,Nps=576
scheme_results.key_recovery_note: exact per target key; per-image disposable keys prevent reuse but not per-image key compromise

paper_targets.title: Pixel-Based Image Encryption without Key Management for Privacy-Preserving Deep Neural Networks
paper_targets.notes: Pixel-wise negative-positive transformation and color-component shuffling family.

paper_targets.title: A Privacy-Preserving Machine Learning Scheme Using EtC Images
paper_targets.authors: Kawamura, Kinoshita, Nakachi, Shiota, and Kiya

paper_targets.title: Privacy-Preserving Image Classification Using an Isotropic Network
paper_targets.notes: EtC/block-wise encryption applied to isotropic networks such as ViT and ConvMixer.

paper_targets.title: Privacy-Preserving Image Classification Using ConvMixer with Adaptive Permutation Matrix
paper_targets.notes: ConvMixer adaptation of block-wise scrambled image encryption with an adaptive permutation matrix.

paper_targets.title: An Encryption Method of ConvMixer Models without Performance Degradation
paper_targets.notes: Secret-key image/model transformation for ConvMixer; image-side transform is reversible under the key.

paper_targets.title: Privacy-Preserving Image Classification Using Vision Transformer
paper_targets.notes: Block scrambling plus in-block pixel-position shuffling for ViT-B/16.

paper_targets.title: Image and Model Transformation with Secret Key for Vision Transformer
paper_targets.notes: Secret-key model/image transformation; image-side operation is patch-level pixel shuffling.

paper_targets.title: Privacy-Preserving Semantic Segmentation Using Vision Transformer
paper_targets.notes: SETR-compatible patch pixel shuffling; preserves patch positions for dense prediction.

paper_targets.title: Effects of JPEG Compression on Vision Transformer Image Classification for Encryption-then-Compression Images
paper_targets.notes: Studies JPEG-compressed EtC images for ViT classification; image transform remains reversible before compression.

paper_targets.title: A Privacy Preserving Method with a Random Orthogonal Matrix for ConvMixer Models
paper_targets.notes: Block-wise random orthogonal matrix transform; included as a reversible linear-key baseline, not a pure permutation.

paper_targets.title: Combined Use of Federated Learning and Image Encryption for Privacy-Preserving Image Classification with Vision Transformer
paper_targets.notes: Federated-learning setting using the same reversible ViT-oriented image encryption family.

paper_targets.title: Efficient Fine-Tuning with Domain Adaptation for Privacy-Preserving Vision Transformer
paper_targets.notes: Same block scrambling plus pixel shuffling, with domain adaptation for utility.

paper_targets.title: Block-Wise Encryption for Reliable Vision Transformer Models
paper_targets.notes: Survey/application paper around block-wise encryption for ViT models.

paper_targets.title: Disposable-key-based Image Encryption for Collaborative Learning of Vision Transformer
paper_targets.notes: Restricted random block/pixel permutation matrices with independent keys per image/client.

paper_targets.title: Privacy-Preserving Vision Transformer Using Images Encrypted with Restricted Random Permutation Matrices
paper_targets.notes: Restricted block and pixel random permutation matrices.

paper_targets.title: Learnable Image Encryption Without Key Management for Privacy-Preserving Vision Transformer
paper_targets.notes: Independent per-client/per-image key family; calibration must match the target key.

paper_targets.title: Privacy-Preserving ConvMixer Without Any Accuracy Degradation Using Compressible Encrypted Images
paper_targets.notes: Compressible encrypted image family adapted for ConvMixer.

qi2022vit_ssim_values_from_figure.metric: SSIM; higher means more visual recovery

chuman2023_jigsaw_attack.note: Reports that a jigsaw-puzzle-based ciphertext-only attack can restore almost all visual information from ViT-oriented block-scrambling/pixel-shuffling encrypted images.

interpretation: All listed permutation/negative-positive/channel-shuffle families are deterministic reversible transforms. With the correct key, reconstruction is exact; with reused pure permutation keys, a chosen calibration image can recover the inverse permutation.
Per-image or disposable-key variants reduce cross-image key reuse, but the protected image for a compromised or calibrated key remains exactly recoverable.
Direct obfuscated-image quality should be reported separately from known-key/chosen-plaintext recovery because ciphertext-only visual quality can look low while exact-key failure remains total.
